issues.summary: Type definition modules import from backend, data, and router layers
evidence: typedef/common.py:2 imports DEFAULT_MAX_COMPLETION_TOKENS from backend.constants
typedef/runs/run_create_base.py:25 imports from backend.constants, :29 from backend.converters, :32 from data.crud_assistant, :33 from routers.supabase_session
typedef/threads/thread_create.py:12 imports from data.crud_message, :13 from data.crud_thread, :14 from routers.supabase_session
typedef/assistants/assistant_types.py:17 imports from backend.rag.index, :19 from data.crud_vector_store, :20 from routers.supabase_session
issues.suggestion: Move constants to a top-level constants module or into typedef. Extract Session into a shared dependencies/ or core/ module. Remove data access and backend logic imports from typedef by moving validation logic into routers or services. Type definitions should be the bottom layer with no upstream dependencies.

issues.summary: backend/composer.py imports from routers/openai/chat.py
evidence: backend/composer.py:37 imports chat_complete and chat_complete_stream_raw from routers.openai.chat
issues.suggestion: Extract the chat completion logic into a shared service module under backend/ (e.g., backend/chat_service.py) that both composer.py and routers/openai/chat.py can depend on. Backend modules should never import from routers.

issues.summary: Data layer modules import from backend security and constants
evidence: data/crud_api_key.py:8 imports APIKey and KEY_PREFIX from backend.security.api_key, :9 imports THIRTY_DAYS_SECONDS from backend.constants
data/crud_vector_content.py:7 imports TOP_K from backend.constants
issues.suggestion: Move shared constants (TOP_K, THIRTY_DAYS_SECONDS) to a neutral location such as a top-level constants module or into the data layer if they are data concerns. Move APIKey validation utilities into a shared security module that sits below or alongside the data layer.

issues.summary: routers/supabase_session.py is imported by 23 files across all architectural layers
evidence: supabase_session.py is imported by typedef modules (run_create_base.py, thread_create.py, message_types.py, assistant_types.py, thread_run_create_params_request.py, run_create.py)
supabase_session.py is imported by backend module composer.py
supabase_session.py is imported by all router modules in routers/openai/ and routers/cowabunga/
issues.suggestion: Extract the Session dependency and authentication utilities into a shared core/dependencies/ module that is independent of routers. This decouples the session type from the router layer and allows all layers to depend on it without creating layer violations.

issues.summary: backend/composer.py is a 403-line hub importing from nearly every module
evidence: composer.py imports from backend.converters, backend.rag.query, data.crud_assistant, data.crud_message, routers.openai.chat, routers.supabase_session, utils.config, cowabunga_sdk.chat.chat_pb2, and multiple typedef modules
composer.py has a re-export ratio of 0.98 but also contains substantial orchestration logic
issues.suggestion: Split composer.py into domain-specific service modules (e.g., chat_service.py, thread_service.py, run_service.py) each owning a cohesive slice of orchestration. Keep composer.py only as a thin facade if necessary, or remove it entirely in favor of direct service usage.

issues.summary: Committed build artifacts duplicate cowabunga_sdk source inside build/lib/
evidence: src/cowabunga_sdk/build/lib/cowabunga_sdk/ contains full copies of __init__.py, llm.py, serve.py, utils.py, cli.py, errors.py, config.py and all protobuf modules
These files are stale copies of source files already present at the package root
issues.suggestion: Delete src/cowabunga_sdk/build/ entirely and add it to .gitignore. Build artifacts should never be committed to source control.

issues.summary: Core backend orchestration and RAG modules have zero test coverage
evidence: composer.py is 403 loc with no unit or integration tests
rag/index.py, query.py, document_loader.py, and leapfrogai_embeddings.py have no corresponding test files
issues.suggestion: Add unit tests for composer helper functions and RAG index/query logic, or integration tests that exercise real (or stubbed) embeddings and indexing

issues.summary: Many data-layer CRUD modules lack dedicated tests
evidence: No test files exist for crud_file_bucket, crud_file_object, crud_message, crud_thread, crud_vector_content, crud_vector_store, or crud_vector_store_file
issues.suggestion: Write focused unit tests for each CRUD module using the existing mock_session pattern, or refactor to shared test fixtures that reduce duplication

issues.summary: Evaluation runners, metrics, and SDK core modules are untested
evidence: niah_runner.py (412 loc) and qa_runner.py (330 loc) have no tests
All metrics files lack tests
SDK modules llm.py, serve.py, and utils.py have no tests
issues.suggestion: Add unit tests for runner orchestration and metric calculations; add smoke tests for SDK serve and utility functions

issues.summary: CRUD unit tests are tightly coupled to Supabase client internals
evidence: test_crud_base.py asserts on mock_session.table(...).insert().execute() call chains
test_crud_api_key.py uses mock_session.table().insert().execute() to verify behavior
Changing the underlying DB client (e.g., from Supabase to Turso) would break these tests even if behavior is identical
issues.suggestion: Refactor CRUD tests to assert on outcomes (return values, raised exceptions) rather than internal Supabase API call sequences, or introduce a repository port/interface to decouple tests from client specifics

issues.summary: Vector store integration tests rely on arbitrary sleeps for expiration
evidence: Line 153: time.sleep(1) to wait for vector store expiration
Line 175: time.sleep(1) to wait for vector store expiration
issues.suggestion: Replace fixed sleeps with deterministic expiration triggers (e.g., mock the clock or use a test-specific TTL of 0 with synchronous cleanup)

issues.summary: E2E background-task test uses fixed polling intervals
evidence: Line 135: time.sleep(2) inside a polling loop waiting for vector store indexing to complete
Test fails if indexing finishes too quickly or too slowly
issues.suggestion: Use an exponential back-off with a short initial delay, or expose a synchronous test hook to await background task completion

issues.summary: API config deletion test relies on a fixed sleep for filesystem detection
evidence: Line 119: time.sleep(0.5) waits for the API to detect a deleted config file
issues.suggestion: Replace the sleep with a retry loop that polls the endpoint until the expected empty response is observed, or trigger a config reload explicitly in tests

issues.summary: CRUD subclasses use inconsistent filter types: some typed models, some raw dicts
evidence: CRUDAssistant, CRUDVectorStore, and CRUDFileObject override get/list/delete to accept typed filter models (FilterAssistant, FilterVectorStore, FilterFileObject)
CRUDThread, CRUDRun, and CRUDMessage inherit base methods directly and pass raw dict filters like {'id': thread_id}
CRUDAPIKey overrides get/list but still accepts dict | None, not a typed filter model
issues.suggestion: Standardize all CRUD subclasses to use typed filter models that convert to dict internally, or remove typed filter subclasses and consistently use dicts across the layer. Pick one pattern and apply it uniformly.

issues.summary: Session parameter position drifts inconsistently across route handlers
evidence: create_assistant(session, request) → session first
create_thread(request, session) → session last
create_run(thread_id, session, request) → session middle
create_message(thread_id, request, session) → session last
delete_vector_store_file(session, vector_store_id, file_id) → session first (unlike siblings in same file)
issues.suggestion: Adopt a consistent parameter ordering convention for all route handlers, such as path parameters first, then session, then request body. Apply consistently across all router modules.

issues.summary: Public functions return union types based on runtime flags rather than separate endpoints
evidence: Composer.generate_response returns Run | StreamingResponse based on request.stream flag
create_run in runs.py returns Run | StreamingResponse based on runtime branching
chat_complete returns either a ChatCompletionResponse or a StreamingResponse based on req.stream
complete in completions.py similarly branches on req.stream with no return type annotation
issues.suggestion: Either split streaming and non-streaming into separate functions with distinct return types, or document the union return type explicitly and ensure callers handle both cases. Adding return type annotations to chat_complete and complete would also help.

issues.summary: Most route handlers do not document error conditions in docstrings despite raising HTTPExceptions
evidence: create_assistant raises HTTPException with 400 and 500 status codes but docstring only says 'Create an assistant'
create_thread raises HTTPException with 400 and 500 but docstring lacks Raises section
Only modify_assistant in assistants.py includes a Raises section documenting HTTPException conditions
grpc_client.py functions call gRPC stubs without documented failure modes or retry contracts
Broad try/except Exception blocks wrap helper calls and re-raise as 500, but these error contracts are invisible to API consumers reading the docstrings
issues.suggestion: Add Raises sections to all public route handler docstrings documenting the HTTPException conditions (status codes and triggers). Document gRPC client failure modes (connection errors, timeout behavior) in grpc_client.py.

issues.summary: CRUDBase.delete catches all exceptions and silently returns False
evidence: CRUDBase.delete contains 'except Exception: return False' which hides database connection errors, permission errors, and syntax errors from callers
issues.suggestion: Let specific exceptions propagate or wrap them in a custom CRUD exception that preserves the original cause. Only return False when the delete genuinely matched zero rows.

issues.summary: Public helper module mixes async and sync functions without clear boundary
evidence: async def recv_completion(stream, model) and async def recv_chat(stream, model) are async SSE generators
def grpc_chat_role(role: str) -> lfai.ChatRole, def read_chunks(file: BinaryIO, chunk_size: int), and def object_or_default(obj, _default) are synchronous utilities
All six functions are exported from the same module surface with no subpackage or naming convention separating async from sync utilities
issues.suggestion: Split backend/helpers.py into two modules (e.g., backend/async_helpers.py and backend/sync_helpers.py) or adopt a naming convention that makes async/sync boundary explicit at the import site.

issues.summary: Database client backends expose unequal query surfaces and different factory parameter names
evidence: TursoQueryBuilder adds neq, gt, gte, lt, lte, order, limit, range beyond the QueryBuilder ABC
SupabaseQueryBuilder only wraps the Supabase query object and does not expose the extra filter methods
TursoClient.create(base_url, auth_token) vs SupabaseClient.create(supabase_url, supabase_key) — parameter names differ even though semantic roles are identical
Callers depending on backend-specific query capabilities will fail silently when the factory returns the other backend
issues.suggestion: Expand the QueryBuilder ABC to include the common filter methods, implement them for both backends, and align factory parameter names (e.g., url and key for both). Alternatively, document which query operations are backend-specific.

issues.summary: Cowabunga tokens router bypasses FastAPI dependency injection used by sibling routers
evidence: count_tokens_route(text: str) hardcodes model config lookup internally instead of accepting model_config: ModelConfig = Depends(get_model_config)
count.py tokens(session, model_config, request) properly injects both session and model_config via Depends
The bypass makes the tokens endpoint less testable and inconsistent with the dependency-injection pattern used across all other route modules
issues.suggestion: Refactor count_tokens_route to accept model_config via Depends(get_model_config), matching the pattern in count.py and the OpenAI routers.

issues.summary: SDK serve() and LLM decorator construct interfaces dynamically at runtime
evidence: serve(o, host='0.0.0.0', port=50051) uses hasattr(o, 'ChatComplete') to decide which gRPC services to register
LLM decorator dynamically creates NewClass with ChatComplete, ChatCompleteStream, Complete, CompleteStream, CountTokens methods at runtime
The effective public API of decorated classes is not statically discoverable; type checkers and IDEs cannot infer the generated interface
issues.suggestion: Define an explicit Protocol or ABC that documents the required methods, and use isinstance checks against the protocol instead of hasattr. This makes the contract statically verifiable and improves IDE support for SDK consumers.

issues.summary: Cowabunga tokens endpoint lacks authentication while sibling endpoints require it
evidence: tokens.py defines @router.post('/count') async def count_tokens_route(text: str) -> int with no session: Session parameter
All other cowabunga endpoints (auth.py, count.py, vector_stores.py, rag.py) declare session: Session
issues.suggestion: Add session: Session parameter to count_tokens_route to enforce authentication consistent with sibling cowabunga endpoints

issues.summary: Cowabunga models endpoint lacks authentication while the equivalent OpenAI endpoint requires it
evidence: models.py defines @router.get('') async def models() with no session: Session parameter
The OpenAI-compliant models.py (src/cowabunga_api/routers/openai/models.py) requires session: Session with comment 'required for authorizing endpoint'
issues.suggestion: Add session: Session parameter to the cowabunga models endpoint for consistency, or document it as intentionally public if that is the design intent

issues.summary: API key validation delegates entirely to database RLS without explicit code-level verification
evidence: _validate_api_authorization does 'response = await session.table("api_keys").select("*").execute()' then checks only 'if not response or not response.data'

The function relies on the RLS policy 'Read only if API key matches and is current' to filter results; Python code never verifies the returned key matches the provided key, checks expiration, or validates user identity
issues.suggestion: Explicitly verify the API key in code by comparing the returned record against the parsed key, checking expiration, and confirming the user_id match, rather than assuming RLS is the sole validation layer

issues.summary: Run step endpoints do not verify run belongs to the provided thread_id while sibling endpoints do
evidence: submit_tool_outputs in runs_steps.py does 'run = await crud_run.get(id_=run_id)' without checking run.thread_id == thread_id
cancel_run in runs_steps.py does 'run = await crud_run.get(id_=run_id)' without checking run.thread_id == thread_id
retrieve_run in runs.py filters by both run_id and thread_id: 'await crud_run.get(filters={"id": run_id, "thread_id": thread_id})'
messages.py retrieve_message, modify_message, and delete_message all filter by both message_id and thread_id
issues.suggestion: Update submit_tool_outputs and cancel_run to verify the retrieved run's thread_id matches the path parameter, consistent with runs.py and messages.py patterns

issues.summary: Multi-line docstrings on trivial one-line getter functions in test utilities
evidence: get_cowabunga_model() has a 6-line docstring for 'return os.getenv("COWABUNGA_MODEL", "vllm")'
get_openai_key() has a 9-line docstring for a 3-line env-var lookup
get_openai_model(), get_cowabunga_api_key(), get_cowabunga_api_url(), get_cowabunga_api_url_base(), openai_client(), cowabunga_client() all repeat the same pattern
issues.suggestion: Replace verbose Google-style docstrings on trivial getters with single-line docstrings or omit them entirely; the types and behavior are self-evident.

issues.summary: Meaningless 'except Exception as e: raise e' blocks in CRUD base methods
evidence: create() wraps result parsing in try/except that catches Exception and immediately re-raises it without modification
list() and update() repeat the same pattern
The only genuine handler is the IndexError branch in get(); the bare 'except Exception as e: raise e' adds no value and obscures the real control flow
issues.suggestion: Remove the no-op except/raise wrappers; keep only the IndexError guard in get().

issues.summary: Identical copy-pasted try/except → HTTPException boilerplate across OpenAI router files
evidence: assistants.py create_assistant: 'except Exception as exc: raise HTTPException(status_code=status.HTTP_400_BAD_REQUEST, detail="Unable to parse assistant request") from exc'
threads.py modify_thread: identical shape with 'Unable to parse thread request'
runs.py modify_run: identical shape with 'Unable to parse run request'
messages.py create_message: identical shape with 'Unable to parse message request'
files.py upload_file: identical shape with 'Failed to parse file'
A second identical pattern for 500-level errors appears in the same files
issues.suggestion: Extract a small helper in the routers package (e.g., raise_parse_error, raise_db_error) or use a FastAPI exception handler to avoid repeating the same 4-line block in every router.

issues.summary: Restating comments echo the code without adding insight
evidence: '# Create .model directory if it doesn't exist' precedes os.makedirs('.model', exist_ok=True)
'# Check if the model is already downloaded' precedes os.path.exists(OUTPUT_FILE)
'# Download the model using huggingface_hub' precedes hf_hub_download(...)
'# Rename to expected output file if different' precedes os.rename(...)
'# Verify checksum if provided' precedes a hashlib check
issues.suggestion: Delete the restating comments; the code is self-explanatory. Keep only comments that explain WHY (e.g., why HF_HUB_ENABLE_HF_TRANSFER is set).

issues.summary: Verbose debug logging dumps large objects inside tight evaluation loops
evidence: logging.info(f'chunk_data: {vector_response["content"]}') inside the chunk enumeration loop dumps full retrieved document contents
logging.info(f'Response message: {response.content[0].text.value}') logs every assistant response verbatim
logging.debug dumps the entire vector-store file list on every iteration
issues.suggestion: Reduce log verbosity in the hot loop; log identifiers and scores at info level, and move full object dumps to a separate debug sink or remove them.

issues.summary: Trivial one-line metric methods wrapped in oversized docstrings
evidence: NIAH_Retrieval.measure() is a 1-liner (self.score = test_case.additional_metadata['retrieval_score']) wrapped in a 19-line docstring with params/returns blocks
NIAH_Response.measure(), NIAH_Chunk_Rank.measure() repeat the same bloat pattern
AnnotationRelevancyMetric.measure() is ~6 lines of simple arithmetic wrapped in a 20-line docstring
issues.suggestion: Trim docstrings to one descriptive line; the code is simpler than the documentation implies.

issues.summary: Unnecessary try/except around a file-open that already raises FileNotFoundError
evidence: The function wraps 'with open(data_path, "r"):' in try/except FileNotFoundError just to re-raise FileNotFoundError with a slightly different message
A restating comment 'Check if the file exists' sits directly above the with-open block
issues.suggestion: Remove the try/except wrapper and the restating comment; let the built-in FileNotFoundError propagate naturally, or use data_path.exists() for an explicit guard.

issues.summary: cowabunga_sdk imported as lfai across API layer — incomplete rebrand from leapfrogai
evidence: 8 production files contain 'import cowabunga_sdk as lfai' or reference lfai.CompletionRequest, lfai.ChatItem, lfai.EmbeddingRequest, etc.
The alias forces every reader to mentally map 'lfai' (leapfrogai) to the current 'cowabunga_sdk' module name.
issues.suggestion: Remove the 'as lfai' alias and use 'cowabunga_sdk' directly (or a domain-neutral alias like 'sdk'). Update all call sites in a single coordinated change.

issues.summary: get_session() backward-compatibility alias has no callers and is documented as legacy shim
evidence: database_factory.py defines get_session() with docstring: 'This function maintains compatibility with existing code that expects a Supabase-like session.'
Zero callers found across the codebase; all consumers invoke create_database_client() directly.
issues.suggestion: Delete the get_session() function and its docstring. No other changes are needed.

issues.summary: leapfrogai_embeddings.py retains old project name in active module path
evidence: Module is named leapfrogai_embeddings.py but implements CowabungaAIEmbeddings class.
Imported by index.py and query.py as 'from cowabunga_api.backend.rag.leapfrogai_embeddings import CowabungaAIEmbeddings'.
issues.suggestion: Rename the file to cowabunga_embeddings.py (or embeddings_client.py) and update the two import sites.

issues.summary: main.py uses lfai_* local aliases for cowabunga routers instead of current naming
evidence: Lines 14-18 define imports: models as lfai_models, vector_stores as lfai_vector_stores, count as lfai_token_count, rag as lfai_rag, tokens as lfai_tokens.
Lines 116-121 register routers using these lfai-prefixed aliases.
issues.suggestion: Rename local aliases from lfai_* to cowabunga_* (e.g., cowabunga_models, cowabunga_vector_stores) to align with the current project name.

issues.summary: TODO comment references obsolete leapfrogai GitHub issue tracker
evidence: converters.py:70 contains '# TODO: What should these numbers be? https://github.com/defenseunicorns/leapfrogai/issues/1110'
issues.suggestion: Update the TODO to reference the current project issue tracker or repository, or resolve the open question and remove the TODO.

issues.summary: src/cowabunga_api/data/ is a flat directory with 12 mixed-concern CRUD files
evidence: Directory contains 12 files with no subdirectories
Entities span auth, assistants, files, messaging, threads, runs, and vector stores
Only crud_message.py, crud_run.py, crud_thread.py share CRUDBase; the rest are unrelated
issues.suggestion: Staged reorg: (1) Create data/base/ and move crud_base.py there. (2) Create data/auth/ and move crud_api_key.py there. (3) Create data/assistants/ and move crud_assistant.py there. (4) Create data/files/ and move crud_file_bucket.py and crud_file_object.py there. (5) Create data/threads/ and move crud_message.py, crud_thread.py, and crud_run.py there. (6) Create data/vectorstores/ and move crud_vector_content.py, crud_vector_store.py, and crud_vector_store_file.py there. (7) Update all internal imports from cowabunga_api.data.crud_* to cowabunga_api.data.<domain>.crud_*. (8) Validate with python -m compileall src/cowabunga_api/data.

issues.summary: Upstream packages typedef/ and data/ import from downstream backend/, creating bidirectional coupling
evidence: typedef/chat_types.py: from ...backend.constants import DEFAULT_MAX_COMPLETION_TOKENS
typedef/assistant_types.py: from cowabunga_api.backend.rag.index import IndexingService
typedef/run_create_base.py: from cowabunga_api.backend.constants import ... and from cowabunga_api.backend.converters import ...
data/crud_api_key.py: from cowabunga_api.backend.security.api_key import APIKey, KEY_PREFIX
data/crud_vector_content.py: from cowabunga_api.backend.constants import TOP_K
backend/composer.py: from cowabunga_api.routers.openai.chat import chat_complete
issues.suggestion: Staged reorg: (1) Move src/cowabunga_api/backend/constants.py to src/cowabunga_api/constants.py and update all imports. (2) Move src/cowabunga_api/backend/security/ to src/cowabunga_api/security/ and update data/crud_api_key.py imports. (3) For backend/converters.py: extract the functions imported by typedef/ into a new src/cowabunga_api/transform/ or src/cowabunga_api/adapters/ package that depends only on typedef/. Update typedef files to import from the new location. (4) In typedef/assistants/assistant_types.py, replace the IndexingService import with a string forward reference or a Protocol defined in typedef/. (5) For backend/composer.py, remove the direct import from routers/openai/chat by moving the shared chat logic into backend/ (e.g., backend/chat_service.py) and have both composer.py and routers/openai/chat.py import from it. (6) Validate with python -m compileall src/cowabunga_api.

issues.summary: Global mutable Config singleton instantiated at module scope
evidence: src/cowabunga_api/utils/__init__.py line 3: config = Config()
src/cowabunga_api/utils/__init__.py line 6-7: get_model_config() returns the module-level instance
src/cowabunga_api/utils/config.py line 51-52: mutable class attributes models={} and config_sources={}
src/cowabunga_api/utils/config.py line 54-55: mutable default dict arguments in __init__
issues.suggestion: Replace the module-level singleton with an explicit initialization function (e.g., create_config()) that returns a fresh instance, or use a proper registry/context manager. If a true singleton is required, use a thread-safe lazy accessor and avoid mutable default arguments.

issues.summary: Heavy ML model loaded at module import time in text-embeddings package
evidence: packages/text-embeddings/main.py line 21-22: model_dir = os.environ.get('COWABUNGA_MODEL_PATH', '.model'); model = INSTRUCTOR(model_dir)
issues.suggestion: Move model initialization into the InstructorEmbedding class __init__ or a lazily-evaluated factory so the module can be imported without triggering file I/O and model loading.

issues.summary: Class-body code executes BackendConfig and Llama initialization at import time
evidence: packages/llama-cpp-python/main.py line 19: backend_config = BackendConfig() inside class body
packages/llama-cpp-python/main.py line 21-22: os.path.exists check that can raise ValueError at class definition time
packages/llama-cpp-python/main.py line 24-28: llm = Llama(...) instantiated inside class body
issues.suggestion: Move backend_config, existence check, and Llama instantiation into the class __init__ method so they run at object construction time rather than module import time.

issues.summary: Module import mutates process-global logging and environment state
evidence: src/cowabunga_api/utils/logging_tools.py line 5: load_dotenv() at module scope
src/cowabunga_api/utils/logging_tools.py line 7-10: logging.basicConfig(level=os.getenv(...)) at module scope
issues.suggestion: Remove load_dotenv() from the module; rely on the application entry point or deployment environment to load dotenv. If a pre-configured logger is needed, expose a configure_logging() function that callers invoke explicitly rather than running side effects on import.

issues.summary: BackendConfig bakes environment variable into class attribute at definition time
evidence: src/cowabunga_sdk/config.py line 46-48: CONFIG_SOURCES = FileSource(file=os.getenv('COWABUNGA_CONFIG_FILE', 'config.yaml'), optional=True) inside class body
issues.suggestion: Evaluate os.getenv inside an override of __init__ or a factory method so the environment can be changed after import without reloading the module. Alternatively, use a property or lazy resolver for CONFIG_SOURCES.

issues.summary: LLM decorator contains four nearly identical completion methods with duplicated logic
evidence: ChatComplete (lines 134-158), ChatCompleteStream (160-197), Complete (199-219), and CompleteStream (221-255) each build a gen stream, consume it, count tokens, determine finish reason, and build a response
_build_gen_stream manually maps ~15 request fields to GenerationConfig one by one (lines 114-131)
Token-counting and finish-reason logic is duplicated across all four methods
issues.suggestion: Extract a shared helper that accepts a stream and request, consumes it, counts tokens, determines finish reason, and returns the response. Use a single data-driven mapping or dataclass conversion for GenerationConfig instead of 15 manual assignments.

issues.summary: NIAH runner class mixes dataset loading, vector store lifecycle, API orchestration, scoring, and cleanup
evidence: __init__ has 13 optional parameters and performs side effects: loads dataset, creates vector store (lines 41-95)
run_experiment is ~140 LOC handling file I/O, assistant creation, thread/run API calls, response parsing, scoring, cleanup, and result collation (lines 97-248)
cleanup, _load_niah_dataset, _create_assistant, _create_vector_store, _delete_file are all distinct responsibilities bundled in one class
issues.suggestion: Split into focused collaborators: a DatasetLoader, a VectorStoreManager, an ExperimentRunner that orchestrates them, and a separate Scorer. Move side effects out of __init__.

issues.summary: TursoAuthClient combines three authentication strategies with user management and session handling
evidence: Single class implements API key auth (_authenticate_api_key), JWT auth (_authenticate_jwt), session auth (_authenticate_session), user creation (create_user), and password sign-in (sign_in) across 371 LOC
set_session both stores tokens in memory and writes to the database
create_api_key generates tokens and persists hashes, mixing cryptography with storage
issues.suggestion: Decompose into focused classes: Authenticator (JWT, API key, session strategies), UserService (create_user, sign_in), and SessionStore. Each should have a single responsibility.

issues.summary: CRUD update pattern is duplicated across subclasses instead of living in CRUDBase
evidence: crud_run.py update (lines 14-29) and crud_vector_store.py update both repeat: data, _count = await self.db.table(...).update(...).eq(...).execute(); _, response = data; if response: del response[0]['user_id']; return self.model(**response[0]); return None
The pattern is identical but not extracted to the base class
issues.suggestion: Move the common update-and-return pattern into CRUDBase.update(), allowing subclasses to override only when they need custom behavior.

issues.summary: Chat router duplicates model lookup and request building across sync and stream methods
evidence: chat_complete (lines 24-52) and chat_complete_stream_raw (lines 55-80) both perform identical model lookup, message-to-chat-item conversion, and ChatCompletionRequest construction
The only difference is the final call (stream_chat_completion vs stream_chat_completion_raw)
issues.suggestion: Extract a shared helper that builds the model backend and gRPC request from a ChatCompletionRequest. Both router methods should call it.

issues.summary: CRUDAPIKey.create generates cryptographic keys, mixing business logic with data access
evidence: create() calls APIKey.generate() from backend.security.api_key (line 67)
It also masks the returned key with a checksum formatter (line 113)
The CRUD layer should not know about key generation algorithms or masking formats
issues.suggestion: Extract API key generation and masking into a dedicated service module (e.g., services/api_key_service.py) that uses CRUDAPIKey for persistence but owns the cryptographic and formatting logic.

issues.summary: Mechanical orphaned flag is incorrect for composer.py
evidence: composer.py is actively imported by src/cowabunga_api/routers/openai/runs.py at line 18

issues.summary: CRUDBase.delete catches all exceptions and returns False, hiding failure reasons from callers

try: return True if result.data else False
except Exception: return False
issues.suggestion: Catch specific expected exceptions (e.g., IndexError for empty result.data) and re-raise unexpected ones so callers can distinguish 'no rows matched' from 'database connection failed'.

issues.summary: get_user_id is typed -> str but can raise IndexError on empty query results without documenting this

user_id: str = result.data[0]['user_id']  // result.data may be empty
issues.suggestion: Add a guard clause to check len(result.data) before indexing, or change the return type to str | None and document the None case.

issues.summary: CRUDAPIKey.get is typed -> APIKeyItem but crashes with IndexError when no rows match

return APIKeyItem(name=response[0]['name'], ...)  // response may be empty
issues.suggestion: Return APIKeyItem | None when no match is found, consistent with CRUDBase.get, and add a guard before indexing response[0].

issues.summary: data_path docstring claims it returns a Path but omits the return type and produces a broken path when filename is None

issues.summary: Pointless try/except wrappers in CRUD base add no error context
evidence: crud_base.py:41 'except Exception as e: raise e' around model construction
crud_base.py:61 'except Exception as e: raise e' around get()
crud_base.py:80 'except Exception as e: raise e' around list()
crud_base.py:100 'except Exception as e: raise e' around update()
crud_base.py:115 'except Exception: return False' around delete()
crud_vector_content.py:56 'except Exception as e: raise e'
crud_api_key.py:118 'except Exception as exc: raise exc'
issues.suggestion: Remove the pointless try/except wrappers. If error translation is needed, catch specific exceptions (e.g., IndexError, ValidationError) and raise a domain-specific exception with context. If not needed, let exceptions propagate naturally.

issues.summary: Exception context lost in count router due to missing 'from' clause
evidence: count.py:33-34 'except Exception as e: raise HTTPException(status_code=500, detail=f"Error counting tokens: {str(e)}")' — no 'from e', so the original traceback and exception type are lost
issues.suggestion: Add 'from e' to the HTTPException raise to preserve the original exception chain for debugging.

issues.summary: Broad except masks HTTPException and re-raises with wrong detail
evidence: assistant_types.py:146-150 catches 'except Exception:' (which includes HTTPException from crud_vector_store.get) and re-raises 'HTTPException(status_code=400, detail="Invalid vector store id was provided")', hiding the original 404 or 500
issues.suggestion: Catch specific exceptions expected from the CRUD call, or catch HTTPException separately and re-raise it unchanged. Only catch broad Exception for unexpected errors and log them.

issues.summary: Bare except silently swallows JWT decode failures
evidence: turso_auth.py:247-251 'try: jwt.decode(...); except: pass' — a bare except swallows all errors including jwt.ExpiredSignatureError, jwt.InvalidTokenError, and even KeyboardInterrupt
issues.suggestion: Catch specific jwt exceptions (jwt.DecodeError, jwt.ExpiredSignatureError) and log the failure instead of silently ignoring it.

issues.summary: file_ids_are_valid catches all exceptions and returns False
evidence: index.py:345-349 'try: ... except Exception: return False' — any error (including AttributeError, TypeError, or database connection failures) is converted to False, making real bugs invisible
issues.suggestion: Catch only the expected exception from the CRUD get() call (e.g., IndexError or a domain-specific NotFoundError). Let unexpected exceptions propagate so they surface as bugs.

issues.summary: Data layer mixes raise, return None, return False, and Result-type error strategies
evidence: crud_base.py: create() raises on Exception, get() returns None on IndexError, delete() returns False on Exception
crud_run.py: update() returns None when response is empty instead of raising
turso_client.py: execute() returns TursoResult(data=[], error=str(e)) instead of raising, diverging from Supabase client behavior
issues.suggestion: Choose a single error strategy for the data layer: either raise domain exceptions for all failures, or consistently return a Result type. Do not mix both across sibling modules.

issues.summary: get_initial_messages_base and get_ending_messages_base construct event strings rather than retrieve existing data

issues.summary: CRUD layer type-hints Supabase AsyncClient instead of generic DatabaseClient
evidence: CRUDBase.__init__ accepts db: AsyncClient (supabase-specific) rather than DatabaseClient
TursoClient implements DatabaseClient but cannot be used by existing CRUDs due to mismatched typing
database_factory.py creates DatabaseClient but callers cast or ignore the abstraction
issues.suggestion: Refactor all CRUD constructors and CRUDBase to accept DatabaseClient, relying on the QueryBuilder interface for queries.

issues.summary: Composer backend module imports chat completion functions from the router layer
evidence: composer.py imports chat_complete and chat_complete_stream_raw from cowabunga_api.routers.openai.chat
This creates a reverse dependency where backend depends on router, violating layer direction
issues.suggestion: Extract chat completion logic into a backend service module (e.g., backend/chat_service.py) and have both composer.py and the chat router depend on it.

issues.summary: Backend Composer raises FastAPI HTTPExceptions directly, coupling backend to HTTP transport
evidence: Composer.list_messages raises HTTPException(status_code=500) on CRUD failure
Backend layer should not know about HTTP status codes
issues.suggestion: Introduce backend-specific exceptions (e.g., BackendError) and map them to HTTPExceptions in the router layer.

issues.summary: Run router prints raw traceback before wrapping exceptions, bypassing structured logging at the router-backend seam
evidence: runs.py catches Exception from Composer and calls traceback.print_exc() before raising HTTPException
This loses structured log context and makes error monitoring unpredictable
issues.suggestion: Replace traceback.print_exc() with logger.exception() and re-raise HTTPException preserving the original cause.

issues.summary: Vector store router mixes high-level service calls with direct CRUD calls
evidence: create_vector_store and modify_vector_store delegate to IndexingService
retrieve_vector_store, list_vector_store_files, and delete_vector_store_file instantiate CRUDs directly
This creates an inconsistent handoff pattern across the router-data seam
issues.suggestion: Consolidate all vector store operations behind a single service facade so the router always delegates to one collaborator.

add.header: Three-package separation is clean at top level
add.description: cowabunga_api, cowabunga_sdk, and cowabunga_evals have distinct responsibilities and minimal cross-package coupling. packages/ service stubs depend on cowabunga_sdk in a single direction.

add.header: Database abstraction supports dual backends
add.description: The data/database layer provides a unified interface for both Supabase (PostgreSQL) and Turso (SQLite/libSQL) through DatabaseClient, SupabaseClient, and TursoClient. This is a well-designed boundary.

add.header: typedef layer imports from all higher layers
add.description: Type definition modules under typedef/ import from backend/, data/, and routers/ across at least 8 files. This is a systemic layer inversion that forces cycle pressure and prevents typedef from being a stable foundation.

add.header: supabase_session.py acts as a cross-layer hub
add.description: The Session type is defined in routers/supabase_session.py but imported by 23 files across typedef, backend, data, and router layers. A router module should not be the provider of a cross-cutting dependency type.

add.header: composer.py aggregates dependencies from nearly every module
add.description: backend/composer.py is a 403-line hub importing from backend, data, routers, typedef, and SDK. It creates a large blast radius for common changes and also violates layer direction by importing from routers.

add.header: Committed build artifacts in cowabunga_sdk/build/
add.description: The build/ directory contains stale copies of the entire SDK source tree. This blurs package boundaries and should be removed with a .gitignore entry.

add.header: Layered test structure mirrors source layout
add.description: The repository maintains separate unit, integration, e2e, and conformance test directories that generally map to the source tree, which aids navigation and coverage targeting.

add.header: Conformance tests validate OpenAI API compatibility
add.description: A dedicated conformance suite checks assistant, chat, file, message, run, thread, vector store, and tool endpoints against OpenAI types, providing a safety net for API contract drift.

add.header: Core backend and RAG modules lack any tests
add.description: The composer, grpc_client, and all RAG submodules (index, query, document_loader, leapfrogai_embeddings) have zero unit or integration tests, leaving critical indexing and retrieval paths unverified.

add.header: CRUD unit tests are locked to Supabase client internals
add.description: Tests for CRUDBase and CRUDAPIKey assert on MagicMock call chains matching the Supabase Python client API; swapping the client implementation would break the tests without changing behavior.

add.header: Timing-dependent sleeps introduce flaky tests
add.description: Integration and e2e tests use fixed time.sleep calls (0.5s, 1s, 2s) to wait for expiration, config reload, or background indexing, making the suite fragile under varying CI load.

add.header: Consistent async router handlers
add.description: All FastAPI route handlers across openai/ and cowabunga/ routers are consistently async, with no sync/async mixing at the router boundary.

add.header: CRUD inheritance pattern provides structural consistency
add.description: Despite filter-typing drift, the CRUDBase generic class gives the data layer a predictable shape (create/get/list/update/delete) that is followed by all subclasses.

add.header: FastAPI dependency injection used consistently
add.description: Session and model_config dependencies are injected via Depends across most route modules, showing a consistent integration pattern with the framework.

add.header: OpenAI typedef models follow consistent Pydantic patterns
add.description: All OpenAI-compatible request/response types use Pydantic BaseModel with consistent field naming and validation, providing a coherent serialization surface.

add.header: Database backends expose unequal query surfaces
add.description: TursoQueryBuilder has filter methods (neq, gt, gte, lt, lte, order, limit, range) that SupabaseQueryBuilder lacks, so callers cannot rely on a uniform query API regardless of which backend is configured.

add.header: SDK uses runtime dynamic dispatch instead of static protocols
add.description: serve() and the LLM decorator use hasattr and runtime class construction, which means the public SDK contract is only discoverable at runtime and cannot be type-checked statically.

add.header: Uniform RLS coverage across all tables
add.description: All 9 application tables in the API schema have RLS enabled with per-user policies, creating a consistent database-level authorization boundary.

add.header: OpenAI router surface consistently requires auth
add.description: Every endpoint in the 12 openai router modules declares session: Session, maintaining a uniform auth boundary for the OpenAI-compliant API surface.

add.header: Manual auth declaration pattern is error-prone
add.description: Auth is enforced by manually adding session: Session to each endpoint rather than via router-level middleware or dependency injection. This explains why two cowabunga endpoints were missed.

add.header: Implicit RLS trust for API key validation
add.description: The application layer assumes RLS policies will validate API keys, so _validate_api_authorization only checks if any rows are returned. This mixed trust boundary means a service_role or admin client bypass would accept any key.

add.header: OpenAI-spec field descriptions in typedef files
add.description: The typedef modules (audio_types.py, file_types.py, etc.) contain detailed Field descriptions that mirror the OpenAI API spec. These are valuable for consumers and are not restating comments.

add.header: Eval runner logging is intentionally verbose
add.description: The niah_runner and qa_eval modules log heavily because they are designed to run as standalone CLI experiments where console visibility matters. The logging density is a deliberate trade-off, not accidental noise.

add.header: CRUD subclasses are thin and focused
add.description: Most CRUD subclasses (CRUDThread, CRUDMessage, CRUDAssistant) contain only an __init__ call and minimal overrides. This is a positive pattern that avoids bloat, making the one outlier (CRUDBase defensive wrappers) more noticeable.

add.header: Persistent lfai alias for cowabunga_sdk
add.description: The SDK module was renamed from leapfrogai to cowabunga_sdk, but 8 production files still import it as 'lfai'. This is an internal-only alias that creates persistent cognitive overhead without serving external compatibility.

add.header: Stale get_session() backward-compatibility shim
add.description: database_factory.py defines get_session() explicitly as backward compatibility, yet zero callers exist. All code uses create_database_client() directly.

add.header: UI JS web components are intentional, not migration residue
add.description: The two .js files in the UI (CodeBlock.js, styles.js) are Lit-based web components with explicit documentation explaining why they are implemented as vanilla JS instead of Svelte/TS. This is not an incomplete TypeScript migration.

add.header: Domain-aligned typedef subdirectories
add.description: The typedef/ directory is well-decomposed into per-resource subfolders (assistants, audio, auth, chat, completion, embeddings, files, messages, models, rag, runs, threads, vectorstores) that mirror OpenAI API resource types, making navigation predictable.

add.header: Clean router decomposition in routers/openai/
add.description: Each OpenAI-compatible endpoint lives in its own file with consistent structure, making the API surface easy to navigate and ownership clear.

add.header: Coupling-direction violation typedef-to-backend
add.description: Multiple typedef files import from backend/ (constants, converters, IndexingService), violating the expected dependency stack where type definitions should be at the bottom with no implementation dependencies.

add.header: Data layer imports backend security and constants
add.description: data/crud_api_key.py imports from backend.security.api_key, and data/crud_vector_content.py imports from backend.constants. The data layer should sit below backend in the dependency stack.

add.header: Database client factory defers env reads to call time
add.description: src/cowabunga_api/utils/database_factory.py reads DATABASE_TYPE and related vars inside create_database_client() rather than at import time, which is a clean pattern that avoids boot-order coupling.

add.header: Test utilities wrap env access in getter functions
add.description: tests/utils/client.py consistently wraps os.getenv calls inside getter functions (get_openai_key, get_cowabunga_api_url, etc.), making test setup independent of import order and allowing easy mocking.

add.header: Global mutable Config singleton at module scope
add.description: src/cowabunga_api/utils/__init__.py instantiates Config() at import time and shares it across ~15 consumers via get_model_config(). The Config class also uses mutable default arguments and mutable class attributes, creating a shared mutable state surface with no encapsulation boundary.

add.header: FastAPI routers follow consistent focused pattern
add.description: OpenAI and Cowabunga router modules each handle a single resource domain with predictable CRUD+exception patterns. This is a positive, maintainable design.

add.header: CRUDBase provides clean generic data access abstraction
add.description: The Generic CRUDBase with TypeVar pattern gives a consistent interface across all data models. Most subclasses are thin and focused.

add.header: LLM decorator duplicates logic across four service methods
add.description: The cowabunga_sdk.llm.LLM decorator adds ChatComplete, ChatCompleteStream, Complete, and CompleteStream with ~80% duplicated token-counting, finish-reason, and response-building logic. This should be extracted into shared helpers.

add.header: NIAH runner conflates six distinct responsibilities
add.description: niah_runner.py handles dataset loading, vector store lifecycle, assistant management, thread/run orchestration, scoring, and cleanup in a single 412 LOC class. This is a clear mixed-responsibility hotspot.

add.header: Turso auth client bundles three auth strategies plus user management
add.description: turso_auth.py mixes API key auth, JWT auth, session auth, user creation, password sign-in, and token generation in one 371 LOC class. Each strategy deserves its own focused module.

add.header: Mechanical orphaned flags are mostly false positives
add.description: The scanner flagged ~44 files as orphaned, but nearly all are actively used via FastAPI router mounting, package __init__ re-exports, test imports, or CLI entry points. This reduces trust in the orphaned detector for this codebase.

add.header: CRUDBase provides a consistent generic interface
add.description: Most data modules inherit from CRUDBase and follow the same create/get/list/update/delete pattern, which keeps contracts predictable across the data layer.

add.header: FastAPI router handlers generally typed
add.description: OpenAI-compliant router handlers in src/cowabunga_api/routers/openai/ mostly declare explicit return types, providing honest API surface contracts.

add.header: Supabase result indexing without guards
add.description: A recurring pattern throughout the data layer is accessing result.data[0] without first checking that the list is non-empty. This creates an implicit IndexError contract that contradicts the declared return types.

add.header: Early-return guard clauses in OpenAI routers
add.description: Routers consistently use `if not model: raise HTTPException` guard clauses before business logic, making control flow easy to follow.

add.header: No dead code after unconditional returns
add.description: Across the production source there is no unreachable code after unconditional `return`, `raise`, or `break`, indicating disciplined control-flow authorship.

add.header: Scattered async-without-await helpers
add.description: Multiple model helpers and converters are declared `async` despite containing no `await`, adding runtime overhead and cognitive load without benefit.

add.header: Three-package top-level decomposition
add.description: src/ is cleanly divided into cowabunga_api (API server), cowabunga_evals (evaluation framework), and cowabunga_sdk (model backend SDK). Each maps to a distinct capability and domain, making high-level navigation straightforward.

add.header: OpenAI domain-aligned router and typedef organization
add.description: routers/openai/ and typedef/ are organized by OpenAI API entity (assistants, chat, completions, embeddings, files, messages, runs, threads, vector_stores). This makes the API surface predictable and mirrors the external contract.

add.header: Database backend abstraction factory
add.description: data/database/ provides a clean factory and abstract base for both Supabase and Turso backends, localizing database backend choice to a single configuration point without leaking into CRUD logic.

add.header: Stale README paths after fork rename
add.description: The README and DEVELOPMENT.md still reference leapfrogai_api/, leapfrogai_evals/, and leapfrogai_sdk/ paths, but the actual directories were renamed to cowabunga_*. This documentation drift undermines trust for new engineers.

add.header: Backend and typedef modules depend on router layer
add.description: backend/composer.py imports from routers/openai/chat.py, and multiple typedef/ modules import Session from routers/supabase_session.py. These cross-layer dependencies violate the expected directionality where routers depend on backend and types, not vice versa.

add.header: Backend source split between src/ and packages/
add.description: Model backend implementations (vllm, whisper, repeater, text-embeddings, llama-cpp-python) live as standalone Python files under packages/ rather than under src/cowabunga_sdk/ or src/cowabunga_api/. This creates two disconnected code universes and makes backend logic hard to discover.

add.header: Pydantic models consistently typed in typedef layer
add.description: All request and response objects in src/cowabunga_api/typedef/ use Pydantic BaseModel with explicit Field types, descriptions, and examples. This creates a strong type foundation for the API surface.

add.header: Modern union syntax used throughout
add.description: The codebase consistently prefers str | None and int | None over Optional[str] and Optional[int], indicating a modern Python typing mindset.

add.header: CRUDBase uses Generic[ModelType] with bounded TypeVar
add.description: src/cowabunga_api/data/crud_base.py correctly uses TypeVar bound=BaseModel and Generic[ModelType], giving precise typing for create/get/list/update/delete operations.

add.header: Internal service layer has annotation gaps
add.description: While routers and Pydantic models are well-typed, the grpc_client.py and composer.py internal service layers omit return types on many public functions, creating a trust boundary where types stop helping.

add.header: Pydantic BaseModel as dominant type strategy
add.description: Nearly all API request/response types and database config classes use pydantic BaseModel. This is a settled, positive convention that keeps serialization and validation behavior consistent across the codebase.

add.header: CRUD prefix convention for data access classes
add.description: The data layer consistently names classes with a CRUD prefix (e.g., CRUDRun, CRUDThread). Most inherit from CRUDBase[ModelType], establishing a predictable pattern for database operations.

add.header: gRPC servicer PascalCase methods are framework-required
add.description: Methods like ChatComplete, CountTokens, CreateEmbedding, Transcribe, and Translate use PascalCase because they override generated gRPC servicer stubs. This is intentional and forced by the protobuf interface, not a naming drift.

add.header: Modern pipe-union typing syntax is codebase standard
add.description: The codebase overwhelmingly uses X | None instead of Optional[X] and list[X] instead of List[X]. Legacy typing imports are outliers.

add.header: Router layer preserves exception chains consistently
add.description: Most OpenAI router modules use 'raise HTTPException(...) from exc' when translating exceptions, preserving the original traceback for debugging. This is a positive, deliberate pattern.

add.header: Data layer lacks a shared error contract
add.description: CRUD modules mix raise, return None, return False, and pointless re-raise patterns without a documented strategy. This inconsistency forces consumers to handle multiple failure shapes.

add.header: Only two custom exception classes in src/
add.description: The entire src/ tree defines only AppImportError and FileAlreadyIndexedError. Everything else relies on generic HTTPException or bare strings, indicating minimal investment in a domain error model.

add.header: Consistent CRUD naming conventions
add.description: All data access modules use crud_ prefix with uniform create/get/list/update/delete method names, making the data layer predictable.

add.header: Descriptive from_*_to_* converter pattern
add.description: Converter functions in converters.py use verbose but clear from_source_to_target naming, eliminating ambiguity about transformation direction.

add.header: Boolean predicate naming discipline
add.description: Functions returning booleans consistently use is_ or can_ prefixes (is_supported_mime_type, can_use_rag), making conditional logic readable.

add.header: id_ convention for avoiding builtin shadowing
add.description: The parameter name id_ is used consistently across CRUD modules to avoid shadowing Python's built-in id, treated as an established convention.

add.header: Intentional typedef package facades
add.description: Every typedef/ subdomain uses an __init__.py to re-export its public types from a *_types.py module. This is a deliberate, bounded pattern that stabilizes import paths without obscuring internals.

add.header: CRUDBase generic abstraction serves 8+ domain types
add.description: CRUDBase is parameterized over ModelType and used by CRUDAssistant, CRUDMessage, CRUDRun, CRUDThread, CRUDVectorStore, CRUDVectorStoreFile, CRUDFileObject, and CRUDApiKey. The abstraction is earned because each concrete class binds a distinct OpenAI domain model to its database table.

add.header: DatabaseClient ABC enables Supabase and Turso backends
add.description: DatabaseClient, QueryBuilder, and AuthClient are abstract interfaces with concrete Supabase and Turso implementations. This is a purposeful abstraction for multi-backend support, not speculative generalization.

add.header: backend/helpers.py contains real gRPC-to-SSE conversion
add.description: recv_completion and recv_chat perform substantial mapping from protobuf streaming responses to Server-Sent Event JSON payloads. These are not pass-through wrappers; they add real translation policy.

add.header: Explicit transitive dependency pinning
add.description: Several packages (nltk, pylibmagic, python-magic, storage3, postgrest) are intentionally pinned as direct dependencies even though they are transitive, with explanatory comments documenting why. This is a healthy pattern that prevents upstream breakage.

add.header: No circular dependency cycles in Python import graph
add.description: A structural scan of the cowabunga_api and related source packages found no circular import cycles, indicating the module graph is clean.

add.header: Undeclared direct dependencies in cowabunga_api
add.description: toml and PyYAML are imported directly in config.py but are not listed in cowabunga_api/pyproject.toml; they happen to be available through transitive dependencies, making the build fragile.

add.header: Duplicate HTML sanitization libraries in UI
add.description: The UI package depends on both dompurify and isomorphic-dompurify, which serve the same purpose. CodeBlock.js uses isomorphic-dompurify while Message.svelte uses dompurify, suggesting inconsistent adoption.

add.header: Router handlers show direct, readable control flow
add.description: OpenAI-compliant routers (threads, messages, embeddings, chat) consistently use early validation, explicit CRUD calls, and clear error boundaries. This pattern is a positive anchor for the codebase.

add.header: CRUD base extraction is balanced and consistent
add.description: The Generic CRUDBase in data/crud_base.py provides a clean, bounded abstraction that all specialized CRUD classes inherit uniformly. Extraction level is appropriate for the layer.

add.header: Backend helpers are focused and single-purpose
add.description: backend/helpers.py contains well-scoped utilities (recv_completion, recv_chat, grpc_chat_role, read_chunks) each doing one thing without side effects.

add.header: Core modules contain monoliths and micro-fragmentation
add.description: SDK decorator (llm.py), evals runner (niah_runner.py), and database client (turso_client.py) suffer from either monolithic inline classes or dataclasses defined inside methods. This inconsistency drags down the overall low-level elegance.

add.header: Dedicated backend converter modules for gRPC seams
add.description: backend/helpers.py and backend/converters.py consistently isolate protobuf-to-OpenAI type translation in one obvious place, making the gRPC backend boundary predictable.

add.header: DatabaseClient abstraction exists for multi-backend support
add.description: data/database/base.py defines a DatabaseClient interface with Supabase and Turso implementations, indicating an intentional seam design, though CRUD layers do not yet honor it.

add.header: Request typedefs double as database orchestrators
add.description: CreateThreadRequest, CreateMessageRequest, and RunCreateParamsRequest encapsulate both validation and DB mutation logic. While this centralizes translation, it couples the typedef package to the data layer and makes the DTOs active collaborators rather than passive boundaries.
